title: T5: UI layer + GitHub Pages deployment
description:
Add all user-facing UI, basic CSS, and GitHub Actions deploy workflow.

**Project root**: /Users/nguyenv/pets/pets
**Depends on**: fuvi-hoz.2, fuvi-hoz.3, fuvi-hoz.4

**Files to create**:
- src/ui.ts
- pets.css
- .github/workflows/deploy-pets.yml

**Files to modify**:
- index.html — add bottom-bar div, dialog, popover div; link pets.css
- src/main.ts — export addPet, deletePet, feedPet, throwBall, getPetAt; call initUI

**pets.css**:
- body, html: margin 0; overflow hidden
- canvas: display block; position fixed; top 0; left 0
- #bottom-bar: position fixed; bottom 0; left 0; right 0; height 48px; background rgba(0,0,0,0.75); display flex; align-items center; gap 12px; padding 0 16px; z-index 10
- #bottom-bar button: font-family monospace; cursor pointer; padding 6px 14px; border-radius 4px
- dialog: background #1e1e2e; color #cdd6f4; padding 20px; border-radius 8px; border none
- #popover: position fixed; background white; border-radius 6px; padding 10px; box-shadow 0 2px 12px rgba(0,0,0,0.2); z-index 20; display none

**ui.ts**:

1. initUI(canvas, { addPet, deletePet, feedPet, throwBall, getPetAt }):
   - Wire 'Add pet' button → dialog.showModal()
   - Wire 'Throw ball' button → throwBall()
   - Wire canvas click → hit-test then showPopover or close popover
   - Wire color <select> to update options when type radio changes

2. Add-pet modal (dog/fox radio, color select, name input):
   - On confirm: addPet({ id: crypto.randomUUID(), name, type, color, x: Math.random() * (canvas.width - 64) + 32, y: canvas.height - 80, hunger: 100 })
   - Validate name not empty before submit

3. Pet popover:
   - position at click coords (clamp to viewport)
   - Feed button → feedPet(pet.id); update dot color live
   - Delete button → deletePet(pet.id); close popover
   - Click outside → close

**main.ts additions**:
- export function addPet(data: PetData): void { const p = makePet(data); pets.push(p); savePets(pets); }
- export function deletePet(id: string): void { pets = pets.filter(p => p.id !== id); savePets(pets); }
- export function feedPet(id: string): void { const p = pets.find(p => p.id === id); if (p) { p.hunger = 100; savePets(pets); } }
- export function getPetAt(ox: number, oy: number): Pet | null { return pets.findLast(p => ox >= p.x && ox <= p.x + SPRITE_W && oy >= p.y && oy <= p.y + SPRITE_H) ?? null; }
- After loadAllSprites resolves: import and call initUI(canvas, { addPet, deletePet, feedPet, throwBall, getPetAt })

**deploy-pets.yml**:
```yaml
name: Deploy pets to GitHub Pages
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm run build
      - uses: peaceiris/actions-gh-pages@v4
        with:
          github_token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          publish_dir: ./dist
```

**Verify**:
- Add dog + fox via modal → appear on canvas
- Click pet → popover; Feed turns dot green; Delete removes it
- Throw ball → pets chase, settle, return to idle
- Reload → pets persist
- Push to main → GitHub Actions deploys

title: T4: Ball physics + all-pets chase
T3: Persistence layer (localStorage)
T2: Game loop + Pet FSM + Renderer
T1: Scaffold apps/pets workspace + download GIF assets
description:
Create the standalone pets Vite+TypeScript project at /Users/nguyenv/pets/pets and download all needed GIF assets from vscode-pets.

**What and why**: Bootstrap the pixel pet mini-game as a standalone repo (not inside fuvi). Vite + TypeScript, pure canvas, no React or framework. Also downloads the 20 GIFs we need from tonybaloney/vscode-pets (MIT licensed).

**Files to create**:
- /Users/nguyenv/pets/pets/package.json
- /Users/nguyenv/pets/pets/tsconfig.json
- /Users/nguyenv/pets/pets/vite.config.ts
- /Users/nguyenv/pets/pets/index.html
- /Users/nguyenv/pets/pets/assets/dog/ (downloaded GIFs)
- /Users/nguyenv/pets/pets/assets/fox/ (downloaded GIFs)

**Implementation steps**:

1. mkdir -p /Users/nguyenv/pets/pets/assets/dog /Users/nguyenv/pets/pets/assets/fox

2. Create package.json:
{
  "name": "pets",
  "version": "0.0.1",
  "private": true,
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "vite",
    "build": "tsc --noEmit && vite build",
    "deploy": "gh-pages -d dist"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "gh-pages": "^6.1.1",
    "typescript": "^5.3.3",
    "vite": "^5.0.0"
  }
}

3. Create tsconfig.json:
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2020",
    "module": "ESNext",
    "moduleResolution": "bundler",
    "strict": true,
    "noUnusedLocals": true,
    "noUnusedParameters": true
  },
  "include": ["src"]
}

4. Create vite.config.ts:
import { defineConfig } from 'vite';
export default defineConfig({ base: '/' });

5. Create index.html — minimal HTML with <canvas id="canvas"> filling the viewport and <script type="module" src="/src/main.ts">.

6. Download GIFs via curl from raw.githubusercontent.com/tonybaloney/vscode-pets/main/media/:
   Dog (to assets/dog/): brown_idle_8fps.gif, brown_lie_8fps.gif, brown_run_8fps.gif, brown_walk_8fps.gif, brown_with_ball_8fps.gif, black_idle_8fps.gif, black_lie_8fps.gif, black_run_8fps.gif, black_walk_8fps.gif, black_with_ball_8fps.gif
   Fox (to assets/fox/): red_idle_8fps.gif, red_lie_8fps.gif, red_run_8fps.gif, red_walk_8fps.gif, red_with_ball_8fps.gif, white_idle_8fps.gif, white_lie_8fps.gif, white_run_8fps.gif, white_walk_8fps.gif, white_with_ball_8fps.gif

7. Run npm install (or pnpm install) from /Users/nguyenv/pets/pets.

8. git init and create initial commit.

**Verify**: npm run dev starts Vite dev server, index.html loads with empty canvas at localhost.

title: T6: Add Vitest + Pet FSM unit tests
description:
Add Vitest as a dev dependency and write unit tests for src/pet.ts.

**Why**: The Pet class contains pure, browser-independent FSM logic (edge clamping, state transitions, ball-chase interruption, onTransition callback, toData round-trip) with non-trivial branching. A wrong threshold or guard produces silent visual bugs. Vitest integrates with Vite with zero config and runs in Node.js without a DOM.

**Depends on**: fuvi-hoz.2

**Files to create**:
- src/pet.test.ts

**Files to modify**:
- package.json — add vitest as devDependency, add test script
- tsconfig.json — add vitest types if needed

**Fix needed first**: src/pet.ts update() defaults canvasW to window.innerWidth. Change default to a numeric constant (e.g. 800) so tests can run in Node.js without window being defined.

**Test cases to cover**:
1. sitIdle → walkLeft/walkRight/sleep transitions (stub Math.random to each threshold)
2. walkLeft → edge clamp: position resets to 0 and state transitions to sitIdle
3. walkRight → edge clamp: position resets to canvasW - imgW and state transitions to sitIdle
4. Ball thrown in non-chase state → immediately transitions to chase
5. Ball thrown while already chasing → no re-entry (state stays chase)
6. Ball becomes inactive while chasing → transitions to idleWithBall with timer 1.5s
7. onTransition callback fires on every transition
8. toData() round-trip: construct Pet from PetData, toData() returns equivalent object

**Verify**: npm test passes all cases.
